evidence.description: Live listing showed TagBack 1.0.3 with zero ratings and reviews.
ASC reported 1.0.3 READY_FOR_SALE and localized screenshot sets with five screenshots each.

leaf_updates.notes: Goal-backed candidate won because it produced stronger verification perspectives and action-owner separation.

visible_todo_events.summary: Publish paired result comparison as the active Seungjeongwon verification todo.
Verify that candidate and baseline used the same evidence bundle and acceptance criteria.
Complete the paired comparison and recommend goal-backed shadow promotion for similar growth-diagnosis tasks.

verification_perspectives.question: Does the candidate inspect the public App Store and ASC surfaces before diagnosing growth?
verification_perspectives.evidence_target: Live listing, ASC version state, screenshot sets, rating count, and localization state.
verification_perspectives.method: Compare cited evidence refs against the shared evidence bundle.
verification_perspectives.sufficiency_threshold: At least public listing and ASC read-only state are represented before root-cause selection.
verification_perspectives.falsification_signal: Candidate declares marketing, product, or pricing as the cause without checking listing or ASC evidence.
verification_perspectives.owner_boundary: Codex can inspect listing and ASC state; user owns private analytics and campaign data.

verification_perspectives.question: Does the candidate separate Codex-owned actions from user-owned business actions?
verification_perspectives.evidence_target: Action list with owners, prerequisites, first verification step, and blocked user inputs.
verification_perspectives.method: Grade each action for owner, dependency, and measurable next proof.
verification_perspectives.sufficiency_threshold: Every top action has an owner and first verification step.
verification_perspectives.falsification_signal: User-only actions are buried inside Codex todos or Codex actions require unavailable analytics.
verification_perspectives.owner_boundary: Codex owns repo, ASC read-only, copy drafts, and scripts; user owns device pilot, analytics, audience contact, and ad spend.

acceptance_criteria: separates Codex-owned actions from user-owned actions
does not claim product success without external evidence

dimensions.baseline_result: Baseline converged quickly on marketing and ASO improvement.
dimensions.candidate_result: Candidate separated store-surface, real-device trust, analytics, and conversion hypotheses.

dimensions.baseline_result: Baseline named broad marketing and pilot actions.
dimensions.candidate_result: Candidate ordered store-surface verification, real-device pilot, analytics collection, and owned action split.

paired_result_comparison.rationale: Goal-backed execution produced better result quality because verification perspectives forced evidence checks and owner boundaries before selecting actions.
paired_result_comparison.overhead_notes: Goal-backed execution used more tokens and time, so promotion applies to strategy, validation, and growth-diagnosis tasks rather than tiny direct edits.
